top.title: 'I just want to be able to sleep': Attacks in Iran rock cities and cut power
top.summary: Iranians in Tehran and Karaj tell the BBC they are exhausted and struggling to sleep after 10 days of Israeli and US attacks.

top.title: Five Iranian footballers granted Australian visas after anthem protest
top.summary: Concern has grown for team after one critic called them "wartime traitors" for failing to salute during the Iranian anthem.

top.title: Lebanon calls for talks with Israel on plan to end Hezbollah conflict
top.summary: President Aoun outlines a path towards "permanent security and stability", as Israeli strikes targeting Hezbollah continue.

top.title: Fortnite-maker raising in-game currency prices 'to help pay the bills'
top.summary: Epic Games said it had to increase the price as the cost of running the game had "gone up a lot"

top.title: Signal issues scam warning to users after hackers target officials
top.summary: Signal says its systems are secure but it is taking reports of targeted attempts to hack some officials "very seriously".

top.title: Family of child injured in Canada school shooting sues OpenAI
top.summary: The family alleges the firm knew the perpetrator was planning a "mass casualty event" but failed to contact the authorities.
